title: Data Injection via Unvalidated Input
description: The system lacks details on input validation practices.  If user-supplied data is not properly sanitized or validated before being used in database queries or other operations, attackers could inject malicious code (e.g., SQL injection) or manipulate data to alter system behavior or exfiltrate sensitive information.
affected_component: All components processing user input (web application, APIs)
attack_vector: Malicious input crafted by an attacker, bypassing input validation controls.
mitigations: Implement strict input validation and sanitization for all user-supplied data.
Use parameterized queries or prepared statements to prevent SQL injection.
Employ a Web Application Firewall (WAF) to filter malicious requests.
